partial_detail: Reversed 2 of 5 defects (unknown-creditor case-fold + EFX bureau-tag branch) -> required passed 16/19, reward 0. 3 remaining f2p still FAILED.
gate_result: BANKED. This 5-defect build gated Sonnet 0/5 and Opus 3/10 — strict-corridor quality. Two additional cross-function-traced mediums (normalize_date slice, parse_money currency-symbol) were prototyped and fully verified but are parked in scratch/seven-defect-variant/ for a possible SEPARATE second normalize task; they are deliberately NOT merged into this banked task (adding them would multiply the solve rate toward 0-1/10 and break correspondence with the banked gate evidence).
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN (identical to clean HEAD: 7 failed / 166 passed / 24 skipped in `python -m pytest tests`; the 7 are the pre-existing rot set test_inbound_calls / test_document_ingestion_readiness / test_ingestion_stale_recovery / test_document_report, none touched by these defects)
litmus_result: PASS. Instruction names the directory and the two modules as localization only (like cre-scoring-latent-4 names facts.py/scoring.py); leaks no function names, exact lines, boundary direction, trigger values, defect count, 1:1 enumeration, or grep-able literals (verified by grep). A strong dev cannot locate any of the five single-token slips without reading and reasoning about the case handling, pattern match-scope, and token-variant coverage across both files; there is no failing-test gradient pointing at any of them.
derivability_audit: All five pass the round-2 derivability rule: the uniquely-correct side of each boundary is fixed by a visible adjacent artifact, so none is a convention-pick. Mix: 2 easy-derivable + 3 medium-derivable, zero convention-picks.
env_note: No task-specific env required. The credit-PDF pipeline suite is deterministic and offline; the JWT_SECRET_KEY baked into loangenus-repo:v1 is sufficient. test_credit_pdf_pipeline.py already passes in the base image. The Xactus sample-PDF tests in test_credit_pdf_upload.py are skipped (Windows-only sample path).

defects.edge_input: a placeholder creditor in non-lower casing, e.g. 'Unknown' / 'N/A'
defects.wrong_output: returns False (placeholder accepted as a real creditor) instead of True
defects.pinned_by: the _UNKNOWN_CREDITOR frozenset a few lines above is entirely lower-case (adjacent).

defects.edge_input: two tradelines, same account, creditor spelled 'Chase' vs 'CHASE'
defects.wrong_output: returns both rows instead of collapsing to one
defects.pinned_by: the sibling dedupe_inquiries (next function) and _pool_tradelines (bureau_router.py) build the identical key WITH .lower() (adjacent + near).

defects.edge_input: a name that begins with a street-suffix token, e.g. 'Steven'
defects.wrong_output: returns True (misclassified as address, dropped) instead of False

defects.edge_input: an Equifax tag written with the EFX abbreviation, e.g. 'EFX-B1'
defects.wrong_output: returns an empty set instead of {'equifax'}
defects.pinned_by: _BUREAU_TAG_MAP maps 'EFX' -> 'equifax' (top of file); the dropped branch orphans a live map entry.

defects.edge_input: a creditor whose name starts with a digit, e.g. '1ST NATIONAL BANK'
defects.wrong_output: returns False (discarded as a numeric line) instead of True
defects.pinned_by: the char class describes a whole numeric/currency line; the adjacent SSN reject is $-anchored.

fairness_audit: Gold tests are pure input: raw strings/lines and plain ParsedTradeline dataclasses in, boolean/set/list-length out; zero mocks, no patched module attributes, so no oracle implementation choice is encoded. Every hidden test references only names that exist at base (the four public helpers, dedupe_tradelines, and ParsedTradeline). Alternative-implementation audit: two materially different correct fixes per defect (e.g. .lower() vs .casefold(); $ anchor vs re.fullmatch; re-add EFX vs match (-B\d*) after normalizing EFX->EQX) all pass, because each test asserts observable behavior, not the specific edit. All five functions are live code, reached from adapters/xactus.py, validation.py, and bureau_router.py during real credit-PDF parsing.
leak_checks: In built image silver-task-latent-credit-normalize: `git log --oneline | wc -l` == 1, `git diff` empty. Full agent-visible suite GREEN at planted state (7 failed / 166 passed / 24 skipped, identical failure set to clean HEAD; 0 new visible failures). No f2p test file present in the agent tree; the gold file enters only via config.json test_patch at grade time. Instruction leaks no function names, lines, directions, values, count, 1:1 enumeration, or grep-able literals (verified by grep).

verification_ladder.static: 10 workspace files, all < 256KB, total ~35KB; Dockerfile pinned FROM, 82 lines, seal present.
verification_ladder.leak_check: git history len 1, git diff empty, pytest green (7f/166p/24s) — PASS
verification_ladder.null: reward 0; all 5 f2p FAILED with per-test lines (no collection errors); 14/19 required passed (all p2p) — PASS
verification_ladder.partial: reverse 2 of 5 defects -> reward 0; 16/19 required passed — PASS

resistance_assessment: Banked at Sonnet 0/5, Opus 3/10 — inside the strict Opus 1-4/10 corridor. No local failing test points at any defect (visible suite green at base+5-defects). All five are single-token boundary slips, each with the correct side pinned by a visible adjacent artifact (2 easy + 3 medium, zero convention-picks). The 5 f2p are mutually disjoint (each defect controls exactly one f2p, no cross-masking — confirmed by a clean-vs-planted diff harness). Config can be trimmed 5->4 by dropping one f2p node id without an image rebuild (oracle reverses all five).
